reviews.review_msg: (For MDSA02 at UTSC) Prof Narine is a great lecturer! Also approachable outside of class and respond to emails quickly. The lectures for the course are fun to watch with all the clips added which makes concepts much easier to understand. Weekly readings can be kinda long tho.

Very informative interesting lectures. Very nice and polite professor outside of class when I explained my corona situation. Responds to emails fairly quick. Course material was interesting. Lectures not mandatory but helped more with the final exam as it was a cumulative exam.
easy going and interesting
best professor ever , gpa boostr , good sense of humour and very kind and helpful
Such an easy course!! GPA booster for sure!!!
He is a good prof. He makes communiction interesting with different  down-to-earth examples. He can also explain things pretty well.  He is also happy to help you with things you don't understand.

I don't EVER write reviews. Not in my 5 years of undergrad but let me tell you how amazing he is. He is not only so sweet and considerate (with a voice that that is so smooth and nice to hear) his lectures are so interesting and he is so understanding. His humour is so dorky and cheesy and I think we all need that in our lives. Definite A.

He looks like Justin Trudeau. Anyway, great class. Now i have further appreciation for films. Course material is quite interesting. 2 papers: one very short and one adequate in length.  STUDY GUIDE PROVIDED FOR EXAMS, but you may see few questions not mentioned on the guide. Tests is on the easier side with lenient marking. def worth,would do again
You will love him gradually, especially share ideas with him, that will be so good.

Really nice and funny but I can't recommend this professor if you are seriously into films. It takes forever for him to respond any emails and he is pretty unorganized with his material. He says we'll have quiz today and then postpones because no time after movie. His assignment outline was outdated from last year. The course is pretty easy though.
I enjoyed taking his classes, and reading the material.

Neil is super nice and super smart. He's very laid back and humble. People love him. You do have to be sure not get too into his Simpson's references and loose track of what points you need to be understanding and paying attention too. Unfortunately, he has left SFU for Toronto. Booooooo. We want him back!
Very, very nice prof. He is very helpful, and he never gets angry!

He's a really relaxed and cheery person, always finding things fascinating or amazing. The course was not overly challenging. Lecture notes are not available online, so go to class! He likes to play a lot of videos and reference things to the Simpsons.

Anil or "Neil" warms my heart. He is so amazing as a teacher and an even better person. I was going to drop the class before meeting him because it was far from home, but upon meeting him, i had to stay and I am glad I did!
Really cool guy, very friendly and laid back! His class was very informative and his personality made you want to attend!

are you guys kidding? this is not what i went to university for. to listen to a prof explain to me why everyone LOVES ipods and how cool they are, and to show me YT clips.  he may've made everyone laugh with his "approachable, youthful" manner but 2 me, he just had nothing interesting to say. I can watch YT on my own time. this is why cmns's a joke
Anil is awesomeeee!!!!! I actually looked forward going to class; hes a funny guy who made lectures interesting and enjoyable. Class is a GPA booster for sure!
he's so hot. just take the class. he's really nice, the class isn't too hard, you'll do great if you attend class and do the readings. he's just so hot. that's all.

a genuinely nice guy, midterm and assignments are not hard. He is very easy going and usually makes the class laugh with all his non-sense comments, we love it though. Very interactive with the class during lecture. Midterm was 2 short essays on a choice of 5 questions based on five articles from our courseware. Easy to tell which will be tested.

Anil is a nice guy, very easy going and an interesting character. He makes sarcastic jokes a lot, some are funny. He tries to show lots of videos in class, not too much reading and the midterm was extremely fair. Not a hard class
His lectures are most often really interesting, and is open for debate when covering material. Jokes are always present during lectures and is really responsive to students
Anil is hilarious. His lectures are fairly interesting, even though he does have a tendency to stray from the material a bit. He's a super nice guy and really willing to help.
No Comments
